title: The balance sheet: assets, liabilities, equity

questions.explanation: The balance sheet is a point-in-time snapshot ('as of' a date) — a stock, not a flow. The income and cash flow statements cover a period.
Accounts receivable due in 30 days converts to cash within a year, so it's a current asset. A building and a patent are non-current assets; a 10-year loan is a non-current liability.
Items are ordered by liquidity — how quickly they turn into cash — with cash listed first.
Retained earnings is an equity item: cumulative profits the business kept rather than paying out as dividends. It links the income statement back to the balance sheet.
Working capital = current assets − current liabilities = 120,000 − 40,000 = 80,000.
Current ratio = current assets ÷ current liabilities = 120,000 ÷ 40,000 = 3.0.
The balance sheet is also called the statement of financial position.
